meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Template: Solve Deployment Bottlenecks Fast
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship production-ready SaaS apps for clients in record time.
content.answer_block: Deployment failures stem from environment drift and configuration bloat. The Antigravity project template eliminates these by standardizing infrastructure-as-code through containerized blueprints. By pairing this with Claude Code, you automate the entire CI/CD pipeline, ensuring your client projects deploy seamlessly from local development to production on every push.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and your terminal is screaming at you with a cryptic deployment error. We have all been there—stuck in the 'it works on my machine' purgatory while a deadline looms. The friction between local development and live production is the silent killer of freelance careers.

But what if deployment wasn't a manual hurdle, but an automated afterthought? The Antigravity project template changes the game by treating your infrastructure as a first-class citizen alongside your source code. By leveraging AI-driven orchestration, you can bypass the configuration nightmare and focus on what actually generates revenue: shipping high-value features.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity
steps.description: Standardize your project structure immediately. This ensures all environment variables and dependencies are decoupled from the host system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the standard Antigravity production-ready template. Include Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and a pre-configured CI/CD pipeline for Vercel/AWS.

steps.title: Configure Environment Parity
steps.description: Stop deployment failures before they happen by enforcing strict environment variable validation at the build level.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust environment validation script that checks for missing keys before the build starts, using the Antigravity schema definitions.

steps.title: Automate Deployment Logic
steps.description: Embed your deployment strategy directly into the codebase so the infrastructure evolves with your application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment automation script compatible with Antigravity that handles database migrations and health checks before swapping traffic.

steps.title: Simulate Production Build
steps.description: Run a local dry-run of your production build to catch runtime errors that won't appear in development mode.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Execute a full production build simulation within the Antigravity container to identify any missing assets or dependency conflicts.

steps.title: Finalize CI/CD Handover
steps.description: Connect your repository to your provider, ensuring the Antigravity template triggers automated tests on every pull request.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a configuration file for GitHub Actions that integrates with the Antigravity template, triggering deployment only if all unit tests pass.

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file that mirrors your production schema; never commit actual secrets, even in private repos.
Use Antigravity's built-in caching layer to reduce build times by up to 60% on repeat deployments.
Run your database migrations as a separate, gated step in your CI/CD pipeline to prevent data corruption during rollbacks.
Treat your logs as ephemeral; pipe them directly to an external service like Axiom or Datadog to keep your container footprint lean.
When using Claude Code, always provide a 'context' file describing your specific architecture to prevent hallucinated configuration patterns.
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, perform a live deploy to a staging environment to verify the health check protocols, then move to production once you've audited the logs.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your infrastructure and start scaling your freelance business with systems that work while you sleep. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
